title: Record a Dataset and Train a Policy
description: Full data collection → training → inference pipeline. Requires completed solo_first_run.

pre_check.instruction: Verify teleop is working before recording. Apply PRE-FLIGHT PROTOCOL: gather any unknown params (robot type, leader/follower arm IDs, cameras) in ONE message, then open a terminal popup running teleop with -y flag. User confirms follower mirrors leader, then Ctrl+C to stop.
pre_check.validation_prompt: Does the follower arm mirror the leader correctly?

on_failure.recovery: Return to solo_first_run tutorial at teleop_ready step

start_recording.instruction: Apply PRE-FLIGHT PROTOCOL: gather all recording params in ONE message (dataset name, task, robot type if unknown, leader arm ID, follower arm ID, episode duration, episode count, cameras, push to Hub). Then construct the full command with all params as CLI flags and open a terminal popup — the terminal will NOT re-prompt for anything.

choose_resume_or_rename.instruction: A dataset with that name already exists. Ask the user what to do.

resume_recording.instruction: Resume the existing dataset. Re-use all pre-flight params already collected. Open terminal popup with same dataset name — new episodes will append.

start_recording_new_name.instruction: Ask user for a new dataset name (one question), then open terminal popup with the new name and all other params unchanged.

verify_recording.instruction: Let's verify the dataset was saved. Check that the dataset directory exists at the path you specified.

validation.rule: Dataset directory must exist at the specified local path OR be visible on HuggingFace Hub

delete_incomplete_and_rerecord.instruction: The previous recording was incomplete. Delete the partial dataset when prompted, then re-record from scratch.

replay_verify.instruction: Replay episode 0 to verify your recording looks correct before training. Only the follower needs to be connected for replay.
replay_verify.validation_prompt: Does the replay look correct? Does the follower arm replicate the recorded motion?

on_failure.message: The recording may have captured incorrect motion — re-record from scratch

choose_finetuning_method.instruction: Your dataset looks great! Now it's time to train a policy. How do you want to finetune?

guidance.solo_hub: RECOMMENDED — Solo Hub is a full web UI with cloud GPUs, model management, W&B monitoring, and one-click HuggingFace push. Supports ACT, SmolVLA, Pi0.5, and GR00T N1.5. No local compute required.
guidance.cli: Train locally on your own machine using the terminal. More control over the environment. Requires a local GPU.

redirect_to_hub_guide.instruction: Activate the solo_hub_guide skill and start the hub_vla_finetune tutorial. It picks up exactly from this point — your dataset is recorded locally and can be pushed to HuggingFace as part of the Hub flow.

choose_policy.instruction: Which policy do you want to train? For beginners, ACT or SmolVLA are recommended.

guidance.smolvla: Good all-rounder. Uses vision + language. Initializes from pretrained smolvla_base.
guidance.act: Fast, low data requirements. Great starting point.
guidance.diffusion: High quality output. Slower to train.
guidance.groot: NVIDIA foundation model. Powerful but complex.

train_smolvla.instruction: Gather training params in pre-flight (ONE message): training steps, output directory, W&B logging (yes/no), push to Hub (yes/no). Defaults to lerobot/smolvla_base checkpoint. Run backgrounded via Shell tool, poll and relay progress.

on_failure.message: Automatically switches to PyAV if TorchCodec unavailable — retry training

train_act.instruction: Gather training params in pre-flight (ONE message): training steps, output directory, W&B logging (yes/no), push to Hub (yes/no). Run backgrounded via Shell tool, poll and relay progress.

on_failure.message: Ask user: resume or provide a new output directory name

train_pi0.instruction: Gather training params in pre-flight (ONE message): training steps, output directory, W&B logging (yes/no), push to Hub (yes/no). Works best with more episodes. Run backgrounded via Shell tool, poll and relay progress.

on_failure.message: Automatically switches to PyAV if TorchCodec unavailable — retry training

train_diffusion.instruction: Gather training params in pre-flight (ONE message): training steps, output directory, W&B logging (yes/no), push to Hub (yes/no). Run backgrounded via Shell tool, poll and relay progress.

on_failure.message: Ask user: resume or provide a new output directory name

train_groot.instruction: Gather training params in pre-flight (ONE message): training steps, output directory, W&B logging (yes/no), push to Hub (yes/no). Run backgrounded via Shell tool, poll and relay progress.

on_failure.message: Tool auto-maps camera names when they don't match policy defaults — retry

training_complete.instruction: Validate via Shell tool: run `ls <output_dir>/` and confirm checkpoint_*/ directories exist.

on_failure.recovery: Check disk space and rerun training

run_inference_check.instruction: Gather inference params in pre-flight (ONE message): policy path (checkpoint dir from training), task description, duration in seconds, teleop override with leader arm (yes/no). Run backgrounded via Shell tool. Tell user to watch the follower arm move autonomously.
run_inference_check.validation_prompt: Does the follower arm move autonomously without you holding the leader?

on_failure.message: Executor will run solo robo --scan via Shell tool to diagnose, then retry inference

scan_motors_diagnostic.instruction: Scan for connected motors to diagnose the connection issue before retrying inference.

on_failure.message: Check USB cable, power (12V follower), and all 3-pin motor cables

tutorial_complete.instruction: Your policy is running autonomously on the robot. You've completed the full record → train → inference pipeline.
